items.briefing:
A phishing kit called Kali365, flagged by the FBI, is being used more widely to steal login credentials — Australian staff should be alert to suspicious emails asking them to sign in to familiar services. Separately, a campaign called DriveSurge has compromised thousands of websites to trick visitors into running fake software updates; if a website suddenly prompts you to install an update, do not proceed.

A new cyber risk tool aimed at board-level oversight has launched in Australia, which may be relevant for governance staff at your organisation. **Practical tip:** Never install software updates prompted by a website — always update software through your device's official settings or IT team.

featured.title: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked
featured.quote: AI-assisted social engineering bypasses security, exposing major platform vulnerabilities to attackers.
